class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkWidgets.TTkModelView.tablewidget.TTkTableWidget.classStyle
documentation: default style

A {py:class}`TTkTableWidget` implements a table view that displays items from a model.

```
    Customer Id     ╿First Name ╿Last Name   ╿Company                         ╿City                ╿
1  │DD37Cf93aecA6Dc │Sheryl     │Baxter      │Rasmussen Group                 │East Leonard        │
╾╌╌┼────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────┤
2  │1Ef7b82A4CAAD10 │Preston    │Lozano      │Vega-Gentry                     │East Jimmychester   │
╾╌╌┼────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────┤
3  │6F94879bDAfE5a6 │Roy        │Berry       │Murillo-Perry                   │Isabelborough       │
╾╌╌┼────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────┤
4  │5Cef8BFA16c5e3c │Linda      │Olsen       │Dominguez, Mcmillan and Donovan │Bensonview          │
╾╌╌┼────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────┤
5  │053d585Ab6b3159 │Joanna     │Bender      │Martin, Lang and Andrade        │West Priscilla      │
╾╌╌┼────────────────┼───────────┼────────────┼────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────┤
6  │2d08FB17EE273F4 │Aimee      │Downs       │Steele Group                    │Chavezborough       │
╾╌╌┴────────────────┴───────────┴────────────┴────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────┘
```

The {py:class}`TTkTableWidget` class is one of the Model/View Classes and is part of TermTk's model/view framework.

{py:class}`TTkTableWidget` implements the methods to allow it to display data provided by models derived from the {py:class}`TTkAbstractTableModel` class.

**Navigation**

You can navigate the cells in the table by clicking on a cell with the mouse,
or by using the arrow keys,
you can also hit Tab and Backtab to move from cell to cell.

**Visual Appearance**

The table has a vertical header that can be obtained using the {meth}`verticalHeader` function,
and a horizontal header that is available through the {meth}`horizontalHeader` function.
The height of each row in the table can be set using {meth}`setRowHeight`;
similarly, the width of columns can be set using {meth}`setColumnWidth`.

They can be selected with {meth}`selectRow` and {meth}`selectColumn`.
The table will show a grid depending on the {meth}`setHSeparatorVisibility` {meth}`setVSeparatorVisibility` methods.

The items shown in a table view, like those in the other item views, are rendered and edited using standard delegates.
However, for some tasks it is sometimes useful to be able to insert widgets in a table instead.
Widgets are set for particular indexes with the setIndexWidget() function, and later retrieved with indexWidget().

By default, the cells in a table do not expand to fill the available space.
You can make the cells fill the available space by stretching the last header section.

To distribute the available space according to the space requirement of each column or row,
call the view's {meth}`resizeColumnsToContents` or {meth}`resizeRowsToContents` functions.

```
param name

the widget's name, normally defined in the constructor or through 

type name

```

{py:class}`TTkContainer` include the layout management through {py:class}`TTkLayout` or one of its implementations.

It is the base class of all the composite widgets like {py:class}`TTkSpinBox`
where a {py:class}`TTkLineEdit` is placed and shown when edited, or in the {py:class}`TTkFrame` and
its extension {py:class}`TTkWindow` where the {py:class}`TTkLayout` is used for the child
widgets placements or the {py:class}`TTkMenuBarLayout`,
the main root widget {py:class}`TTk` inherit the layout capabilities of {py:class}`TTkContainer` for any widget placed in the [pyTermTk](https://github.com/ceccopierangiolieugenio/pyTermTk) app.

Main widgets subclassing {py:class}`TTkContainer`:

1. {py:class}`TTk`
2. {py:class}`TTkFrame`
3. {py:class}`TTkWindow`
4. {py:class}`TTkSplitter`
5. {py:class}`TTkAppTemplate`
6. {py:class}`TTkTabWidget`
7. {py:class}`TTkTreeWidget`

More info and examples are available in the {ref}`Tutorial <Layout-Tutorial_Intro>`

(container-layout-topology)=

{py:class}`TTkContainer` Layout topology:

```
Terminal area (i.e. XTerm) = TTk
┌────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                            │
│   TTkContainer                             │
│       < --------  width  ----->            │
│ (x,y)╔═════════════════════════╗  ^        │
│      ║      padt (Top Padding) ║  |        │
│      ║    ┌───────────────┐    ║  | height │
│      ║padl│ Layout        │padr║  |        │
│      ║    └───────────────┘    ║  |        │
│      ║      padb (Bottom Pad.) ║  |        │
│      ╚═════════════════════════╝  v        │
└────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

Example:

```python
from TermTk import TTk, TTkButton, TTkHBoxLayout

# The class TTk, that could be referred as the terminal layout, is a subclass of TTkContainer
root = TTk( layout=TTkHBoxLayout(), padding=(1,2,4,8))

TTkButton(parent=root, border=True,  text="Btn 1!")
TTkButton(parent=root, border=True,  text="Btn 2!")
TTkButton(parent=root, border=True,  text="Btn 3!")
TTkButton(parent=root, border=True,  text="Btn 4!")

root.mainloop()
```

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkWidgets.TTkPickers.colorpicker.TTkColorButtonPicker.colorSelectedBG
documentation:
This signal is emitted when a color is chosen (with the "OK" button in the {py:class}`TTkColorDialogPicker`)

This is a convenience signal that mrrors {py:class}`colorSelectedFG` providing a background color instead.

```
param bgColor

the Background Color

type  bgColor

```

{py:class}`TTkColorButtonPicker` is a button widget that spawn, if pressed, a {py:class}`TTkColorDialogPicker` can be used to choose a {py:class}`TTkColor`.

Quickstart:

```python
import TermTk as ttk

root = ttk.TTk()

btn = ttk.TTkColorButtonPicker(
        parent=root,
        size=(8,3),
        border=True,
        color=ttk.TTkColor.RED )

lfg = ttk.TTkLabel(parent=root, pos=(0,3), text="Test FG")
lbg = ttk.TTkLabel(parent=root, pos=(0,4), text="Test BG")

btn.colorSelectedFG.connect(lfg.setColor)
btn.colorSelectedBG.connect(lbg.setColor)

root.mainloop()
```

inner_classes.name: TermTk.TTkGui.textdocument.TTkTextDocument._snapDiff
documentation: Undo,Redo Logic
Old:
_snapshotId: = last saved/undo/redo state
3 = doc4
_snapshots:
[doc1, doc2, doc3, doc4, doc5, doc6, . . .]
New:
SnapshotId:
Snapshots:                  _lastSnap     _dataLines (unstaged)
Cursors:
c0,   c1,   c2,   c3,   c4 = _lastCursor
Diffs:
[   d01,  d12,  d23,  d34   ] = Forward  Diffs
[   d10,  d21,  d32,  d43   ] = Backward Diffs
Slices: = common txt slices between snapshots
Data Structure
║   Snapshot B  ║          ┌─────────────>║   Snapshot C  ║
║ _nextDiff     ║──────┐   │              ║ _nextDiff     ║───> Next snapshot
┌───║ _prevDiff     ║      │   │          ┌───║ _prevDiff     ║  or Null if at the end
V              A           V   │          V
║   Diff B->A   ║  │  ║   Diff B->C   ║   ║   Diff C->B   ║
║ slice = txtBA ║  │  ║ slice = txtBC ║   ║ slice = txtBA ║
║ snap          ║  │  ║ snap          ║   ║ snap          ║

The grid layout allows an automatic place all the widgets in a grid, \
the empty rows/cols are resized to the "rowMinHeight,columnMinWidth" parameters ``` TTkGridLayout ┌┐ columnMinWidth ╔═════════╤═════════╤╤═════════╗ ║ Widget1 │ Widget2 ││ Widget3 ║ ║ (0,0) │ (0,1) ││ (0,3) ║ ╟─────────┼─────────┼┼─────────╢ ┐ rowMinHeight ╟─────────┼─────────┼┼─────────╢ ┘ ║ Widget4 │ ││ ║ ║ (2,0) │ ││ ║ ╟─────────┼─────────┼┼─────────╢ ║ │ ││ Widget5 ║ ║ │ ││ (3,3) ║ ╚═════════╧═════════╧╧═════════╝ ``` ``` param int columnMinWidth the minimum width of the column, optional, defaults to 0 param int rowMinHeight the minimum height of the column, optional, defaults to 0 ```

methods.name: TermTk.TTkGui.clipboard.TTkClipboard.text
documentation: text

TTkClipboard

{ref}`Clipboard`

Example:

```python
from TermTk import TTkClipboard

# Initialize the clipboard manager
clipboard = TTkClipboard()

# Push some text to the clipboard
clipboard.setText("Example")

# Get the text from the clipboard
text = clipboard.text()
```

```
param int row

(used only in the ), the row of the grid,   optional, defaults to None

param int col

(used only in the ), the col of the grid,   optional, defaults to None

(used only in the ), the rows used by this, optional, defaults to 1

param int colspan

(used only in the ), the cols used by this, optional, defaults to 1

param layoutItemType

The Type of this class, optional, defaults to TTkK.NONE

type  layoutItemType



param alignment

The alignment of this item in the layout (not yet used)

methods.name: TermTk.TTkLayouts.layout.TTkLayout.setGeometry
documentation: setGeometry

The {py:class}`TTkLayout` class is the base class of geometry managers. \
It allows free placement of the widgets in the layout area. \
Used mainly to have free range moving {py:class}`TTkWindow` because the widgets are not automatically rearranged after a layout event ``` ╔════════════════════════════╗ ║ pos(4,2) ║ ║ ┌───────┐ pos(16,4) ║ ║ │Widget1│ ┌─────────┐ ║ ║ │ │ │ Widget2 │ ║ ║ │ │ └─────────┘ ║ ║ │ │ ║ ║ └───────┘ ║ ║ ║ ╚════════════════════════════╝ ```

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkGui.textcursor.TTkTextCursor.MoveMode.MoveAnchor
documentation: Moves the anchor to the same position as the cursor itself.

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkGui.textcursor.TTkTextCursor.MoveMode.KeepAnchor
documentation: Keeps the anchor where it is.

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkGui.textcursor.TTkTextCursor.SelectionType.Document
documentation: Selects the entire document.

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkGui.textcursor.TTkTextCursor.SelectionType.BlockUnderCursor
documentation: Selects the block of text under the cursor.

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkGui.textcursor.TTkTextCursor.SelectionType.LineUnderCursor
documentation: Selects the line of text under the cursor.

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkGui.textcursor.TTkTextCursor.SelectionType.WordUnderCursor
documentation: Selects the word under the cursor. If the cursor is not positioned within a string of selectable characters, no text is selected.

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkGui.textcursor.TTkTextCursor.MoveOperation.NoMove
documentation: Keep the cursor where it is

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkGui.textcursor.TTkTextCursor.MoveOperation.Start
documentation: Move to the start of the document.

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkGui.textcursor.TTkTextCursor.MoveOperation.StartOfLine
documentation: Move to the start of the current line.

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkGui.textcursor.TTkTextCursor.MoveOperation.StartOfBlock
documentation: Move to the start of the current block.

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkGui.textcursor.TTkTextCursor.MoveOperation.StartOfWord
documentation: Move to the start of the current word.

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkGui.textcursor.TTkTextCursor.MoveOperation.PreviousBlock
documentation: Move to the start of the previous block.

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkGui.textcursor.TTkTextCursor.MoveOperation.PreviousCharacter
documentation: Move to the previous character.

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkGui.textcursor.TTkTextCursor.MoveOperation.PreviousWord
documentation: Move to the beginning of the previous word.

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkGui.textcursor.TTkTextCursor.MoveOperation.Up
documentation: Move up one line.

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkGui.textcursor.TTkTextCursor.MoveOperation.Left
documentation: Move left one character.

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkGui.textcursor.TTkTextCursor.MoveOperation.WordLeft
documentation: Move left one word.

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkGui.textcursor.TTkTextCursor.MoveOperation.End
documentation: Move to the end of the document.

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkGui.textcursor.TTkTextCursor.MoveOperation.EndOfLine
documentation: Move to the end of the current line.

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkGui.textcursor.TTkTextCursor.MoveOperation.EndOfWord
documentation: Move to the end of the current word.

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkGui.textcursor.TTkTextCursor.MoveOperation.EndOfBlock
documentation: Move to the end of the current block.

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkGui.textcursor.TTkTextCursor.MoveOperation.NextBlock
documentation: Move to the beginning of the next block.

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkGui.textcursor.TTkTextCursor.MoveOperation.NextCharacter
documentation: Move to the next character.

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkGui.textcursor.TTkTextCursor.MoveOperation.NextWord
documentation: Move to the next word.

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkGui.textcursor.TTkTextCursor.MoveOperation.Down
documentation: Move down one line.

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkGui.textcursor.TTkTextCursor.MoveOperation.Right
documentation: Move right one character.

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkGui.textcursor.TTkTextCursor.MoveOperation.WordRight
documentation: Move right one word.

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkGui.textcursor.TTkTextCursor.MoveOperation.NextCell
documentation: Move to the beginning of the next table cell inside the current table. If the current cell is the last cell in the row, the cursor will move to the first cell in the next row.

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkGui.textcursor.TTkTextCursor.MoveOperation.PreviousCell
documentation: Move to the beginning of the previous table cell inside the current table. If the current cell is the first cell in the row, the cursor will move to the last cell in the previous row.

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkGui.textcursor.TTkTextCursor.MoveOperation.NextRow
documentation: Move to the first new cell of the next row in the current table.

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkGui.textcursor.TTkTextCursor.MoveOperation.PreviousRow
documentation: Move to the last cell of the previous row in the current table.

methods.name: TermTk.TTkTemplates.keyevents.TKeyEvents.keyEvent
documentation:
This event handler, can be reimplemented in a subclass to receive key events for the widget.

:::{note}
Reimplement this function to handle this event
:::

```
param evt

The keyboard event

type evt



return

True if the event has been handled

rtype

bool
```
def keyPressEvent(self, evt:TTkKeyEvent) -> bool :
This event handler, can be reimplemented in a subclass to receive key press events for the widget.
.. note:: Reimplement this function to handle this event
:param evt: The keyboard event
:type evt: :py:class:`TTkKeyEvent`
:return: **True** if the event has been handled
:rtype: bool
return False
def keyReleaseEvent(self, evt:TTkKeyEvent) -> bool :
This event handler, can be reimplemented in a subclass to receive key release events for the widget.
.. note:: Reimplement this function to handle this event
:param evt: The keyboard event
:type evt: :py:class:`TTkKeyEvent`
:return: **True** if the event has been handled
:rtype: bool
return False

classes.name: TermTk.TTkAbstract.abstracttablemodel.TTkModelIndex

methods.name: TermTk.TTkAbstract.abstracttablemodel.TTkModelIndex.row
documentation:
Returns the row this model index refers to.

```
return

int
```

methods.name: TermTk.TTkAbstract.abstracttablemodel.TTkModelIndex.col
documentation:
Returns the column this model index refers to.

```
return

int
```

methods.name: TermTk.TTkAbstract.abstracttablemodel.TTkModelIndex.data
documentation:
Returns the data for the item referred to by the index.

```
return

object
```

methods.name: TermTk.TTkAbstract.abstracttablemodel.TTkModelIndex.setData
documentation:
Set the data in the item referred by the current index.

```
param data

the data to be set in the (row,col) position of the table

type data

object
```

This class is used as an index into item models derived from {py:class}`TTkAbstractTableModel`.
The index is used by item views, delegates, and selection models to locate an item in the model.

New {py:class}`TTkModelIndex` objects are created by the model using the {py:class}`TTkAbstractTableModel` -> {meth}`~TermTk.TTkAbstract.abstracttablemodel.TTkAbstractTableModel.index` function.
An invalid model index can be constructed with the {py:class}`TTkModelIndex` constructor.

Model indexes refer to items in models, and contain all the information required to specify their locations in those models.
Each index is located in a given row and column; use {meth}`row`, {meth}`column`, and {meth}`data` to obtain this information.

To obtain a model index that refers to an existing item in a model, call {py:class}`TTkAbstractTableModel` -> {meth}`~TermTk.TTkAbstract.abstracttablemodel.TTkAbstractTableModel.index` with the required row and column values.

methods.name: TermTk.TTkAbstract.abstracttablemodel.TTkAbstractTableModel.setData
documentation:
Returns true if successful; otherwise returns false.

The {meth}`~TermTk.TTkAbstract.abstracttablemodel.TTkAbstractTableModel.dataChanged` signal should be emitted if the data was successfully set.

The base class implementation returns false. This function and {meth}`data` must be reimplemented for editable models.

```

param col

methods.name: TermTk.TTkAbstract.abstracttablemodel.TTkAbstractTableModel.headerData
documentation:
Returns the data for the given role and section in the header with the specified orientation.

For horizontal headers, the section number corresponds to the column number.
Similarly, for vertical headers, the section number corresponds to the row number.

```

the orienttin of the header to be retrieved

type orientation

```

methods.name: TermTk.TTkAbstract.abstracttablemodel.TTkAbstractTableModel.flags
documentation:

The base class implementation returns a combination of flags that

and allows it to be selected ({py:class}`~TermTk.TTkCore.constant.TTkConstant.ItemFlag.ItemIsSelectable`).

```

int

the column position of the data

type col

int

return

```

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkAbstract.abstracttablemodel.TTkAbstractTableModel.dataChanged
documentation:
This signal is emitted whenever the data in an existing item changes.

If more items are affected, the pos/size definne the minimum area including all of those changes.

When reimplementing the {meth}`setData` function, this signal must be emitted explicitly.

```
param pos

the topLeft margin of the modified area

type pos

tuple(int,int)

param size

the size of the modified area

type size

tuple(int,int)
```

{py:class}`TTkAbstractTableModel` provides a standard interface for
models that represent their data as a two-dimensional array of items.
It is not used directly, but must be subclassed.

Since the model provides a more specialized interface than {py:class}`TTkAbstractItemModel`,
it is not suitable for use with tree views.

The {meth}`rowCount` and {meth}`columnCount` functions return the dimensions of the table.

**Subclassing**

When subclassing {py:class}`TTkAbstractTableModel`, you must implement {meth}`rowCount`, {meth}`columnCount`, and {meth}`data`.
Well behaved models will also implement {meth}`headerData`.

Editable models need to implement {meth}`setData`.

**Built-In Implementation**

{py:class}`TTkTableModelList` basic subclass implementing a 2d list as data structure

{py:class}`TTkTableModelCSV` subclass of {py:class}`TTkTableModelList` including the api to import csv data

{py:class}`TTkTableModelSQLite3` subclass of {py:class}`TTkTableModelList` including support for [sqlite3](https://www.sqlite.org) databases

classes.name: TermTk.TTkAbstract.abstractscrollview.TTkAbstractScrollViewInterface

methods.name: TermTk.TTkAbstract.abstractscrollview.TTkAbstractScrollViewInterface.viewFullAreaSize
documentation:
This method returns the full widget area size of the {py:class}`TTkAbstractScrollViewInterface` implementation.

This is required to `TTkAbstractScrollArea` implementation to handle the on-demand scroll bars.

:::{note}
Reimplement this function to handle this event
:::

```
return

the full area size as a tuple of 2 int elements (width,height)

rtype

tuple[int,int]
```
Override this function

methods.name: TermTk.TTkAbstract.abstractscrollview.TTkAbstractScrollViewInterface.viewDisplayedSize
documentation:
This method returns the displayed size of the {py:class}`TTkAbstractScrollViewInterface` implementation.

:::{note}
Reimplement this function to handle this event

This method is already implemented in the following classes:

- {py:class}`TTkAbstractScrollView`
- {py:class}`TTkAbstractScrollViewLayout`
- {py:class}`TTkAbstractScrollViewGridLayout`
:::

Unless a different iplementation is required, by default it should return {py:meth}`TTkWidget.size`

```
return

the displayed size as a tuple of 2 int elements (width,height)

rtype

tuple[int,int]
```
Override this function

methods.name: TermTk.TTkAbstract.abstractscrollview.TTkAbstractScrollViewInterface.viewMoveTo
documentation:
This method is used to set the vertical and horizontal offsets of the {py:class}`TTkAbstractScrollViewInterface`

:::{note}
Reimplement this function to handle this event

This method is already implemented in the following classes:
: - {py:class}`TTkAbstractScrollView`
  - {py:class}`TTkAbstractScrollViewLayout`
  - {py:class}`TTkAbstractScrollViewGridLayout`
:::

```
param x

the horizontal position

type x

int

param y

the vertical position

type y

int
```

methods.name: TermTk.TTkAbstract.abstractscrollview.TTkAbstractScrollViewInterface.getViewOffsets
documentation:
Retrieve the vertical and horizontal offsets of the {py:class}`TTkAbstractScrollViewInterface`

:::{note}
Reimplement this function to handle this event

This method is already implemented in the following classes:
: - {py:class}`TTkAbstractScrollView`
  - {py:class}`TTkAbstractScrollViewLayout`
  - {py:class}`TTkAbstractScrollViewGridLayout`
:::

```
return

the (x,y) offset

rtype

tuple[int,int]
```

The {py:class}`TTkAbstractScrollViewInterface` provide the basic interface that can be used in {py:class}`TTkAbstractScrollArea` to enable on-demand scroll bars.

When subclassing {py:class}`TTkAbstractScrollViewInterface`,
you must implement {meth}`viewFullAreaSize`, {meth}`viewDisplayedSize`, {meth}`getViewOffsets`, and {meth}`viewMoveTo`.

This interface is implemented in the following specialised classes:

- {py:class}`TTkAbstractScrollView`
- {py:class}`TTkAbstractScrollViewLayout`
- {py:class}`TTkAbstractScrollViewGridLayout`

class_variables.name: TermTk.TTkAbstract.abstractscrollview.TTkAbstractScrollView.viewChanged
documentation:
This signal is emitted whenever there is a change in the view content topology (size,pos)

:::{note}
This signal must be implemented in any implementation
of {py:class}`TTkAbstractScrollView` to notify that the view content boudaries changed
:::

The {py:class}`TTkAbstractScrollView` is a {py:class}`TTkContainer` widget that incude {py:class}`TTkAbstractScrollViewInterface` api.

The placement of any widget inside this container will change accordingly to the offset of this view.

This class is used in the convenience widget {py:class}`TTkScrollArea`

classes.name: TermTk.TTkUiTools.uiloader.TTkUiLoader

methods.name: TermTk.TTkUiTools.uiloader.TTkUiLoader.loadFile
documentation:
load the file generated by [ttkDesigner]

```
param filePath

the file path

type filePath

str

param baseWidget

the custom widget that will be extended with this ui definition, if not defined a new  will be returned,defaults to None

type baseWidget

, optional

param kwargs

the custom initialization args,defaults to None

type kwargs

dictionary, optional

return

```

methods.name: TermTk.TTkUiTools.uiloader.TTkUiLoader.loadJson
documentation:
load the json representing the ui definition of the widget

```
param text

the representation of the widget in Json format

type text

json generated by ttkDesigner

param baseWidget

the custom widget that will be extended with this ui definition, if not defined a new  will be returned,defaults to None

type baseWidget

, optional

param kwargs

the custom initialization args,defaults to None

type kwargs

dictionary, optional

return

```

methods.name: TermTk.TTkUiTools.uiloader.TTkUiLoader.loadDict
documentation:
load the dictionary representing the ui definition of the widget

```
param ui

the representation of the widget

type ui

dictionary generated by ttkDesigner

param baseWidget

the custom widget that will be extended with this ui definition, if not defined a new  will be returned,defaults to None

type baseWidget

, optional

param kwargs

the custom initialization args,defaults to None

type kwargs

dictionary, optional

return

```

TTkUiLoader

[ttkdesigner Tutorial](https://github.com/ceccopierangiolieugenio/pyTermTk/blob/main/tutorial/ttkDesigner/textEdit)

[ttkdesigner]: https://github.com/ceccopierangiolieugenio/pyTermTk/tree/main/tools/ttkDesigner
